refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct obligations: (1) deriving requirements from context, (2) recording them, and (3) managing them over time. Derivation is the primary functional obligation in the problem domain; recording and managing are implementation concerns that belong in the DP/IM layers. The refined FR focuses on the core functional need: translating worksite mission inputs into explicit performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability). This remains solution-neutral and testable against worksite inputs.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that given a worksite mission context (e.g., site layout, production targets, safety constraints), the system produces a documented set of performance requirements that are traceable to those inputs and accepted by the Site Manager and Safety Manager.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall accept worksite mission context inputs (e.g., production targets, site constraints, safety policies).
CR-NGHE-005.2: The NGHE solution shall derive quantitative performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from worksite mission context.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The NGHE solution shall establish traceability between derived performance targets and source worksite inputs.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The NGHE solution shall maintain a baseline record of performance targets and calibration updates.
refined_fr.note: The refined FR is atomic at the derivation level. However, the acceptance criteria and stakeholder needs indicate that recording and managing baselines/calibration history are also functional obligations. Sub-FRs 005.1–005.4 decompose the full scope into atomic, solution-neutral functional statements. Each sub-FR can be independently verified and mapped to a DP and IM.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement a mission-context capture module that accepts worksite inputs (safety plan, operational policies, environmental conditions) and applies a rule-based inference engine to derive performance targets; maintain a versioned baseline repository with change tracking.

DP-B: Establish a hierarchical performance requirement decomposition process where worksite mission context is mapped to system-level performance targets through a documented traceability matrix; store all versions in a configuration management system.

DP-C: Design a parameterized performance model that accepts worksite mission context as input variables and computes performance targets via a calibrated mathematical model; log all model inputs, outputs, and calibration events.

DP-D: Create a manual performance requirement specification workflow where domain experts review worksite context and document performance targets in a structured template; implement a change control board to approve and version all updates.

DP-E: Implement a hybrid approach combining automated context parsing (DP-A logic) with expert validation gates (DP-D oversight) to derive performance targets; maintain dual-layer traceability (automated derivation + expert approval) and full audit history.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-E: Implement a hybrid approach combining automated context parsing with expert validation gates to derive performance targets; maintain dual-layer traceability (automated derivation + expert approval) and full audit history.

potential_sources: Coupling between context capture module and traceability matrix if schema changes are not versioned independently.
Coupling between performance model and baseline repository if model updates are not decoupled from historical baseline storage.
Coupling between expert validation workflow and automated inference if approval logic is hardcoded rather than configurable.
coupling_check.notes: DP-E mitigates coupling by separating concerns: (1) automated derivation logic is isolated from (2) expert validation gates, and (3) traceability/audit is orthogonal to both. The dual-layer approach allows independent evolution of inference rules and approval policies. Risk: if validation gate criteria are tightly bound to specific performance model assumptions, decoupling may be compromised. Mitigation: define validation gate criteria as independent configuration, not embedded in model code.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context inputs are available in a structured or semi-structured format (e.g., safety plan document, operational policy database). RISK: If inputs are unstructured narrative text, automated parsing will require NLP or manual pre-processing, increasing complexity and error rate.

ASSUMPTION: Expert validators are available and responsive to approve derived performance targets within acceptable lead time. RISK: If validation becomes a bottleneck, the system may not meet operational agility requirements.

ASSUMPTION: Performance targets derived from mission context are deterministic or have well-defined uncertainty bounds. RISK: If mission context is ambiguous or contradictory, the system may produce conflicting or non-traceable targets.

ASSUMPTION: Worksite safety plan and operational policies are maintained in a controlled configuration. RISK: If policies change frequently without formal change control, traceability to performance targets may become stale or incorrect.
ASSUMPTION: Baseline and calibration history is required for compliance or audit purposes. RISK: If retention policy is not defined, storage and retrieval of historical data may become unmanageable.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: FR-NGHE-005 (refined): The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context via a hybrid process that (1) automatically parses worksite inputs (safety plan, operational policies, environmental conditions) to generate candidate performance targets, (2) subjects candidates to expert validation and approval, (3) produces a documented, versioned set of performance targets with full traceability to source inputs and approval records, and (4) maintains a queryable history of all baselines, calibration updates, and policy changes. Acceptance criteria: (a) Every performance target is traceable to at least one worksite input and one expert approval record. (b) All baseline and calibration updates are logged with timestamp, actor, and rationale. (c) System supports rollback to any prior baseline version. (d) Traceability reports are generated on demand and include derivation chain and validation evidence.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Centralized module responsible for performance target derivation, validation gate management, dual-layer traceability (automated + expert approval), and audit history maintenance for safety-critical performance parameters.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational context data (vehicle state, environmental conditions, mission parameters) required for automated context parsing and performance target derivation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Outputs validated performance targets and operational constraints derived from hybrid analysis to guide vehicle/teleoperation execution.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional: receives safety-critical events for re-validation; outputs alerts when expert validation gates reject automated derivations or audit anomalies detected.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Receives expert validation inputs and approval decisions from remote safety oversight systems; transmits audit trail and traceability records for compliance verification.

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent fit: DP-E is fundamentally a safety validation and audit mechanism. Automated context parsing, expert validation gates, dual-layer traceability, and audit history are all core safety governance functions. Minor diffusion: Telemetry ingestion and Tasking output are necessary but external dependencies, not diffusion of core logic.

Moderate fit: NGHE is a broad solution-level container. DP-E could reside here, but would lack focused ownership and would diffuse across multiple sub-systems (vehicle control, teleoperation, safety). Creates unclear accountability for validation gate enforcement and audit trail integrity.

Poor fit: Vehicle module is primarily concerned with physical actuation and state management. Performance target derivation and expert validation are orthogonal to vehicle-level concerns. Placing DP-E here would scatter safety governance logic across vehicle firmware, creating tight coupling and audit trail fragmentation.

Poor fit: Teleoperation focuses on remote command execution and operator interface. Expert validation gates and audit history are safety governance concerns, not teleoperation concerns. High diffusion: would require teleoperation to manage safety traceability, violating separation of concerns.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Safety Services is the natural owner of DP-E because: (1) automated context parsing for performance targets is a safety-critical derivation function; (2) expert validation gates are inherently safety governance mechanisms; (3) dual-layer traceability and audit history are compliance and safety assurance artifacts; (4) Safety Services already owns SafetyAlert and is the logical hub for safety-critical decision validation. Containment is strong because all core logic (parsing, validation, traceability, audit) remains within Safety Services boundaries, with only necessary external data flows (Telemetry in, Tasking out, expert inputs via Network Link).
containment_risks: Risk: Expert validation inputs may arrive asynchronously or be delayed, requiring Safety Services to manage temporal consistency of dual-layer traceability. Mitigation: implement explicit state machine for validation gate lifecycle (pending → approved/rejected → audit-logged).
Risk: Audit history volume could grow unbounded, creating storage and query performance issues. Mitigation: define retention policies and implement tiered archival within Safety Services.

Risk: Automated context parsing logic may require domain-specific knowledge (vehicle dynamics, mission constraints) that resides in Vehicle or Teleoperation modules. Mitigation: Safety Services should consume parsed context via Telemetry interface rather than reimplementing parsing; keep parsing logic in source modules, validation in Safety Services.

Risk: Network Link may introduce latency in expert validation, creating gaps in real-time performance target updates. Mitigation: implement fallback to conservative (safe) defaults while awaiting expert approval; log all fallback decisions in audit trail.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: DP-E is well-formulated for Safety Services containment. No revision needed. However, clarify the boundary between 'automated context parsing' (which may be distributed across Vehicle and Teleoperation) and 'expert validation + traceability' (which Safety Services owns). Refine DP-E to emphasize that Safety Services owns the validation gate logic and audit trail, while consuming pre-parsed context from Telemetry. This preserves cohesion and minimizes diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-E correctly addresses FR-NGHE-005 by specifying a hybrid approach (automated + expert validation) that derives performance targets from worksite mission context. The dual-layer traceability and audit history directly satisfy the acceptance criterion requiring documented, traceable performance targets with baseline/calibration history. The DP is sufficiently detailed to guide implementation and is grounded in safety governance principles.

diffusion_risks: Automated context parsing logic may be distributed across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules (source of truth for domain-specific constraints), while Safety Services owns validation and traceability. Risk: unclear ownership of parsing correctness and consistency. Mitigation: establish explicit contract—Safety Services consumes pre-parsed context via Telemetry interface; Vehicle/Teleoperation own parsing logic and correctness; Safety Services owns validation gate enforcement.

Expert validation inputs arrive asynchronously via Network Link, creating temporal consistency risk in dual-layer traceability. Risk: performance targets may be applied before expert approval is recorded, or approval may arrive after targets are superseded. Mitigation: implement explicit validation gate state machine (pending → approved/rejected → audit-logged) with fallback to conservative defaults during approval latency.

Audit history volume and query performance could degrade if retention policies are not defined. Risk: Safety Services containment becomes brittle under scale. Mitigation: define tiered archival strategy and retention policies within Safety Services; implement efficient indexing for compliance queries.

Network Link latency may prevent real-time expert validation, creating gaps in performance target updates. Risk: system may operate on stale or unapproved targets. Mitigation: implement explicit fallback logic (conservative safe defaults) while awaiting approval; log all fallback decisions in audit trail for compliance review.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Safety Services is the correct owner (containment score 0.92). All core logic—expert validation gates, dual-layer traceability, audit history maintenance—are safety governance functions that belong in a centralized, auditable module. External interfaces (Telemetry in, Tasking out, SafetyAlert bidirectional, Network Link in) are necessary data flows, not diffusion of core logic. Minor diffusion risk exists only at the boundary between automated context parsing (which may originate in Vehicle/Teleoperation) and validation (which Safety Services owns). This is manageable via clear interface contracts and is preferable to scattering validation logic across multiple modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. Acceptance criteria are directly verifiable: (1) documented performance targets can be traced to worksite inputs via Telemetry interface and audit trail; (2) baseline/calibration history is maintained in Safety Services audit logs; (3) expert validation gates are logged with timestamps and approver identity; (4) compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies can be verified by querying audit trail for policy-conformant derivations. Traceability is end-to-end: worksite mission context (Telemetry) → automated parsing → expert validation (Network Link) → approved targets (Tasking) → audit record (Safety Services).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear. Safety Services is the single owner of DP-E realization. Responsibilities are well-defined: (1) ingest operational context from Telemetry; (2) apply automated derivation logic (consuming pre-parsed context); (3) enforce expert validation gates via Network Link; (4) maintain dual-layer traceability (automated derivation + expert approval); (5) generate audit history; (6) output validated targets to Tasking; (7) emit SafetyAlert on validation rejection or audit anomalies. Ownership boundaries are unambiguous, and accountability for validation gate enforcement and audit integrity is localized to Safety Services.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-E to explicitly separate concerns: 'Safety Services shall implement expert validation gates and dual-layer traceability for performance targets derived from worksite mission context. Automated context parsing is consumed from Telemetry (sourced by Vehicle/Teleoperation); Safety Services owns validation gate logic, approval workflow, and audit trail maintenance.' This clarifies the DP<->IM boundary and prevents diffusion of parsing logic into Safety Services.

Define explicit validation gate state machine in DP-E: states (pending approval, approved, rejected, audit-logged), transitions (triggered by expert input via Network Link or timeout), and fallback behavior (conservative safe defaults during approval latency). This addresses temporal consistency risk and ensures audit trail completeness.

Specify audit trail retention and archival policy as part of DP-E: define retention periods for different audit record types (derivation logs, approval records, fallback decisions), archival triggers, and query performance requirements. This mitigates unbounded growth risk.

Add explicit interface contract for Telemetry: specify the schema and semantics of pre-parsed context (vehicle state, environmental conditions, mission parameters) consumed by Safety Services. This ensures parsing correctness is owned by source modules and validation correctness is owned by Safety Services.

Document fallback logic for Network Link latency: specify conservative safe defaults applied when expert approval is delayed, and require all fallback decisions to be logged in audit trail with timestamps and reason codes. This ensures compliance visibility and supports post-hoc validation of safety decisions.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass, confirming that FR-NGHE-005 is well-formed and DP-E is architecturally sound. However, the gate analysis identifies five concrete diffusion risks and temporal consistency hazards that require explicit refinement of DP-E before proceeding to sub-FR decomposition. The recommended revisions are not cosmetic; they address safety-critical concerns (validation gate state machine, fallback logic, audit trail completeness) that must be captured in the DP specification to ensure correct implementation and verifiability. Revising DP-E now prevents downstream rework and ensures that any sub-FRs derived from FR-NGHE-005 inherit a well-defined, risk-mitigated parent specification.

revise.why: DP-E must be refined to address five critical gaps identified in the gate analysis: (1) explicit separation of automated context parsing (owned by Vehicle/Teleoperation, consumed via Telemetry interface) from validation gate logic (owned by Safety Services); (2) formal validation gate state machine (pending → approved/rejected → audit-logged) with explicit transitions and timeout handling; (3) fallback behavior specification for Network Link latency (conservative safe defaults, logged with reason codes); (4) audit trail retention and archival policy to prevent unbounded growth and ensure query performance; (5) explicit interface contract for Telemetry schema and pre-parsed context semantics. These refinements are mandatory for a safety-critical system and must be captured in DP-E before sub-FR decomposition proceeds.

assumptions: Automated context parsing logic is sourced from Vehicle/Teleoperation modules and is pre-validated before ingestion by Safety Services via Telemetry interface.
Expert validation inputs arrive asynchronously via Network Link with variable latency (no hard real-time guarantee).
Safety Services is the single authoritative owner of validation gate enforcement, dual-layer traceability, and audit trail maintenance.
Fallback to conservative safe defaults is acceptable during expert approval latency, provided all fallback decisions are logged for compliance review.
Audit trail retention policies can be defined within Safety Services without requiring cross-module coordination.
Telemetry interface schema and pre-parsed context semantics can be formally specified as a contract between source modules (Vehicle/Teleoperation) and consumer (Safety Services).
open_risks: Validation gate state machine timeout behavior: if expert approval does not arrive within a defined window, system must apply conservative defaults. Timeout duration and default target values must be specified in DP-E to ensure safety compliance.

Audit trail query performance under scale: if performance targets are derived frequently and audit records accumulate rapidly, query latency for compliance verification may degrade. Indexing strategy and archival policy must be defined in DP-E.

Temporal consistency between automated derivation and expert approval: if targets are applied before expert approval is recorded, or approval arrives after targets are superseded, audit trail may not reflect actual safety decisions. Validation gate state machine must enforce strict ordering.
Network Link availability: if Network Link is unavailable for extended periods, expert validation cannot be performed. Fallback logic and escalation procedures must be specified in DP-E.

Parsing correctness ownership: if automated context parsing logic is distributed across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, consistency and correctness may be difficult to verify. Interface contract must clearly assign ownership and define pre-conditions for parsed context.
evidence_needed: Formal specification of validation gate state machine: state diagram, transition conditions, timeout handling, and fallback behavior.
Audit trail retention and archival policy: retention periods by record type, archival triggers, and query performance requirements.
Telemetry interface contract: schema definition, pre-parsed context semantics, validation rules, and ownership of parsing correctness.
Fallback logic specification: conservative safe default values, conditions for fallback activation, logging requirements, and compliance review procedures.
Network Link latency profile: expected latency distribution, timeout thresholds, and escalation procedures for approval delays.
Safety Services module interface specification: input/output contracts for Telemetry, Network Link, Tasking, and SafetyAlert; error handling and edge cases.
